text.title: TypeScript Strict Patterns
text.summary: Type narrowing, branded types, as const, assertion functions, discriminated unions, and Zod IO boundaries.

rule.statement: Use type guards (is X), discriminated unions, and instanceof/in narrowing. Never use as to bypass narrowing.
rule.rationale: Narrowing is verified by the compiler; as casts bypass strictness and hide real bugs.

rule.statement: Use branded types for IDs and other string/number values that must not be mixed. This prevents passing OrderId where UserId is expected.
rule.rationale: Without branding, all string IDs are structurally identical; passing the wrong ID is a silent runtime bug.

rule.statement: Use as const for literal objects and arrays that serve as enum sources or discriminant values.
rule.rationale: Without as const, TypeScript widens "INCREMENT" to string; as const preserves the literal type for discriminated unions.

rule.statement: Use assertDefined() and assert() with asserts return type to narrow without non-null assertion (!).
rule.rationale: ! suppresses the error without communicating intent; assertion functions throw with a message and narrow the type for downstream code.

rule.statement: Validate all external data (HTTP request bodies, env vars, JSON files, localStorage) with Zod at the boundary. Downstream code receives fully typed values.
rule.rationale: External data is always unknown at runtime; Zod converts it to typed values and provides actionable error messages on parse failure.
